title: Syntactic Belief Update as the Driver of Garden Path Processing Difficulty
abstract: arXiv:2606.27206v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Garden path sentences present a processing difficulty for humans -- the sentence prefix leads the listener towards one interpretation, until the listener hears a critical word that shows that the initial interpretation was wrong. Lexical surprisal, a measure that usually predicts sentence processing difficulty quite well, fails to provide good predictions for garden path sentences.   We propose an alternative that actively predicts a probability distribution over syntactic trees (its syntactic belief) and updates that distribution after each new word. If a processor is led down a garden path, syntactic beliefs will be wrong and will require a large update at the critical word. The magnitude of the update is measured with a generalized R\'enyi divergence. Crucially, this metric is dependent on lexical items, but is fully independent of the probability of lexical items. This Syntactic Belief Update provides a better fit to the human readin

translated_title: 句法信念更新作为花园小径句处理困难的驱动因素
translated_abstract: 花园小径句会给人类带来处理困难——句子的前缀引导听者朝向一种解释，直到听者听到一个关键词语，表明最初的解释是错误的。词汇惊奇度这一通常能较好预测句子处理难度的指标，却无法为花园小径句提供良好的预测。我们提出了一种替代方案，该方案主动预测句法树上的概率分布（即其句法信念），并在每个新词后更新该分布。如果处理过程被引向花园小径，句法信念将会出错，并需要在关键词语处进行大幅更新。这种更新的幅度通过广义Rényi散度来衡量。关键在于，该指标依赖于词汇项，但完全独立于词汇项的概率。这种句法信念更新能更好地拟合人类的阅读时间数据。
tldr: 本文提出句法信念更新机制，通过广义Rényi散度度量句法信念在关键词语处的大幅更新，从而有效解释花园小径句的处理困难，优于传统词汇惊奇度指标。
en_tdlr: This paper proposes a syntactic belief update mechanism that measures large updates at critical words via generalized Rényi divergence, effectively explaining garden path processing difficulty and outperforming traditional lexical surprisal.
